content.assessment: This proposal leverages the ministry's demonstrated discretionary approval power, most clearly evidenced in the Tiszavasvári refusal, which shows an existing administrative lever operating without new legislation and explicitly invoking segregation concerns (F-live-hungarian_education_system-09). This gives the mechanism strong institutional fit for the state sector. However, the three-track system itself, including its coexistence since the early 1990s (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01) and the centrally graded uniform exam, remains fully unchanged for whichever places persist, meaning the admission criteria's differential access effects (documented by parental-education gaps, F-live-hungarian_education_system-04) are preserved for surviving capacity. System coverage is also a critical limitation from this lens: because ministerial discretion as exercised has been directed at specific institutional cases rather than systemic capacity, and because the underlying legal framework for the tracks is untouched, this remains a partial, case-by-case tool rather than a structural contraction mechanism.
opportunities: Could be combined with the ministry's stated agenda to review the entrance-exam system, since both operate through existing administrative/policy channels rather than new legislation (F-live-hungarian_education_system-10)
strengths: Grounded in a real, already-exercised administrative precedent (Tiszavasvári refusal) demonstrating institutional feasibility without new statute (F-live-hungarian_education_system-09)
threats: The three parallel track lengths were themselves introduced as institutional add-ons (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01), suggesting the underlying legal structure has proven durable and may resist administrative-only contraction over time
weaknesses: Admission criteria and track structure for any remaining places are untouched, so the composition effects tied to parental education (F-live-hungarian_education_system-04) and coaching markets (F-live-hungarian_education_system-05) persist unabated for whoever remains admitted
